result.summary: Adyen exposes HTTP APIs for Checkout, BinLookup, Disputes, and Webhooks plus an MCP server; authenticated surfaces use Adyen API keys or webhook HMAC, while the MCP auth mechanics couldn’t be confirmed from available docs.

adyen_api_key.label: Adyen API key
adyen_api_key.setup: In the [Adyen Customer Area](https://ca-test.adyen.com/ca/ca/config/api_credentials.shtml), create an API credential and generate an `API key`. Copy it once and store it securely; Adyen uses this key for server-to-server API authentication.

adyen_hmac.label: Adyen webhook HMAC key
adyen_hmac.setup: In the [Adyen Customer Area](https://ca-test.adyen.com/ca/ca/config/webhooks.shtml), open a webhook configuration and generate the `HMAC key` used to sign and verify webhook payloads.
